title: carex buxbaumii subsp. subulata
intro: Carex buxbaumii subsp. subulata, a subspecies of the sedge plant, is a type of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its diuretic and antiseptic effects, which can aid in treating urinary tract infections and skin conditions. The health benefits of Carex buxbaumii subsp. subulata include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water, and it can be consumed as a tea or applied topically as a poultice.
